messages.content: You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como migrar VMs VMware on-premises para OCI Compute?

Para migrar VMs VMware on-premises para OCI Compute:

**Pré-requisitos:**
1. Criar VCN com subnets no OCI
2. Configurar connectivity (VPN ou FastConnect)
3. Preparar VMs para migração

**Método 1: Exportar OVF/OVA e importar no OCI**
```
# 1. Exportar VM do VMware
# Via vSphere Client: Export → Export OVF Template
# Ou via CLI:
ovftool vi://user@vcenter/MinhaVM ./minha-vm.ova

# 2. Converter para formato compatível com OCI
# OCI suporta VMDK, QCOW2, VDI
tar xvf minha-vm.ova
# Extrai .vmdk files

# 3. Upload para OCI Object Storage
oci os object put \
  --bucket-name migration-bucket \
  --name minha-vm.vmdk \
  --file ./minha-vm-disk1.vmdk

# 4. Importar como custom image
oci compute image import \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "vmware-migrated-vm" \
  --source-image-type VMDK \
  --launch-mode PARAVIRTUALIZED \
  --source-uri "https://objectstorage.us-ashburn-1.oraclecloud.com/n/<namespace>/b/migration-bucket/o/minha-vm.vmdk"

# 5. Criar instância OCI
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex \
  --source-details '{"sourceType": "image", "imageId": "ocid1.image.oc1..exampleID"}'
```

**Método 2: OCI Application Migration Service**
1. Console: Solutions & Platform → Application Migration
2. Configurar source VMware
3. Mapear para OCI shapes
4. Executar migração

**Preparar VM VMware:**
```
# Instalar drivers VirtIO antes de exportar
# Linux:
sudo apt-get install -y cloud-init cloud-utils

# Windows:
# Instalar VirtIO drivers do Fedora project
# https://fedorapeople.org/groups/virt/virtio-win/direct-downloads/
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como converter VMDK para formato compatível com OCI?

Converter VMDK para formato OCI:

**Formatos suportados pelo OCI:**
- VMDK (diretamente suportado)
- QCOW2
- VDI
- OCI

**Se seu VMDK não é compatível:**

**1. Verificar tipo de VMDK:**
```
qemu-img info minha-vm.vmdk
# Verificar se é monolítico ou split
```

**2. Converter VMDK split para monolítico:**
```
qemu-img convert -f vmdk -O vmdk -o subformat=streamOptimized \
  minha-vm.vmdk minha-vm-converted.vmdk
```

**3. Converter para QCOW2:**
```
qemu-img convert -f vmdk -O qcow2 minha-vm.vmdk minha-vm.qcow2
```

**4. Otimizar imagem:**
```
# Zerar espaço livre antes de exportar
# Linux:
sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/tmp/zero bs=1M || true
sudo rm /tmp/zero

# Windows:
sdelete -z C:

# Compactar
qemu-img convert -c -f vmdk -O vmdk minha-vm.vmdk minha-vm-compressed.vmdk
```

**5. Upload e importação:**
```
oci os object put \
  --bucket-name migration-bucket \
  --name minha-vm.vmdk \
  --file ./minha-vm-converted.vmdk

oci compute image import \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "converted-vm" \
  --source-image-type VMDK \
  --launch-mode PARAVIRTUALIZED \
  --source-uri "https://objectstorage.us-ashburn-1.oraclecloud.com/n/<namespace>/b/migration-bucket/o/minha-vm.vmdk"
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/preparingimage.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como planejar migração lift-and-shift de datacenter on-premises para OCI?

Planejar migração lift-and-shift on-premises → OCI:

**Fase 1: Discovery e Assessment (2-4 semanas)**
1. Inventory completo:
   - VMs (count, OS, CPU, memory, storage)
   - Aplicações e dependências
   - Databases e middleware
   - Network topology
2. Classificar workloads:
   - Tier 1: críticos (migração primeiro)
   - Tier 2: importantes
   - Tier 3: podem ser descomissionados
3. Mapear para OCI:
   - VMs → OCI Compute shapes
   - Storage → Block Volumes / Object Storage
   - Network → VCN design

**Fase 2: Landing Zone (1-2 semanas)**
1. Criar VCN com topologia equivalente
2. Configurar IAM policies e compartments
3. Estabelecer connectivity (VPN/FastConnect)
4. Configurar monitoring e logging

**Fase 3: Migração por waves**
```
Wave 1: Non-production (validação)
Wave 2: Dev/Test
Wave 3: Staging
Wave 4: Production (por aplicação)
```

**Fase 4: Cutover por wave**
```
# Para cada VM:
# 1. Exportar do VMware/Hyper-V
ovftool vi://vcenter/VM ./vm.ova

# 2. Upload para OCI
oci os object put --bucket-name migration --file vm.vmdk

# 3. Importar como image
oci compute image import --source-image-type VMDK ...

# 4. Launch instance
oci compute instance launch --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex ...
```

**Fase 5: Validação e otimização**
- Testar aplicações
- Otimizar shapes (direito-sizing)
- Implementar autoscaling

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como resolver incompatibilidade de hardware virtual ao migrar VMs on-premises para OCI?

Incompatibilidades de hardware virtual on-premises → OCI:

**Problemas comuns:**

**1. Drivers de storage/network:**
OCI usa VirtIO para paravirtualização.
```
# Linux: verificar drivers
lsmod | grep virtio
lsmod | grep virtio_blk
lsmod | grep virtio_net

# Instalar drivers VirtIO
sudo apt-get install -y cloud-init cloud-utils
sudo yum install -y cloud-init cloud-utils-growpart
```

**2. Kernel sem suporte a VirtIO:**
```
# Verificar configuração do kernel
zcat /proc/config.gz | grep VIRTIO
# Ou
grep VIRTIO /boot/config-$(uname -r)

# Deve mostrar:
# CONFIG_VIRTIO_BLK=y
# CONFIG_VIRTIO_NET=y
```

**3. Boot mode incompatível:**
```
# OCI suporta UEFI e BIOS
# Ao importar, especificar launch-mode
oci compute image import \
  --launch-mode PARAVIRTUALIZED \
  --source-image-type VMDK
```

**4. Windows VMs:**
```
# Instalar VirtIO drivers antes de migrar
# Download: https://fedorapeople.org/groups/virt/virtio-win/
# Instalar: viostor, NetKVM, Balloon

# Ou usar OCI Windows Platform Image como base
# e migrar apenas dados
```

**5. Se a VM não bootar:**
```
# Recovery mode
# 1. Detach boot volume
oci compute boot-volume-attachment detach \
  --boot-volume-attachment-id ocid1.bootvolumeattachment.oc1..exampleID

# 2. Attach como data volume
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..recoveryID \
  --volume-id ocid1.bootvolume.oc1..exampleID \
  --type paravirtualized

# 3. Investigar
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/recovery
cat /mnt/recovery/var/log/boot.log
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/preparingimage.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como migrar configurações de rede on-premises para OCI VCN?

Migrar rede on-premises para OCI VCN:

**Mapeamento de conceitos:**
- On-premises VLAN → OCI Subnet
- On-premises router → OCI Route Table
- On-premises firewall → OCI Security Lists / NSGs
- On-premises NAT → OCI NAT Gateway

**Criar VCN equivalente:**
```
# 1. Criar VCN
oci network vcn create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --cidr-block "10.0.0.0/16" \
  --display-name "migrated-vcn" \
  --dns-label "migratedvcn"

# 2. Criar subnets
oci network subnet create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --cidr-block "10.0.1.0/24" \
  --display-name "public-subnet" \
  --dns-label "public"

oci network subnet create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --cidr-block "10.0.2.0/24" \
  --display-name "private-subnet" \
  --dns-label "private"

# 3. Internet Gateway
oci network internet-gateway create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "igw" \
  --is-enabled true

# 4. NAT Gateway
oci network nat-gateway create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "nat-gw"

# 5. Route Tables
oci network route-table create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "public-rt" \
  --route-rules '[{
    "destination": "0.0.0.0/0",
    "network-entity-id": "ocid1.internetgateway.oc1..exampleID"
  }]'

# 6. Security Lists
oci network security-list create \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "web-servers" \
  --ingress-security-rules '[{
    "protocol": "6",
    "source": "0.0.0.0/0",
    "tcpOptions": {"destinationPortRange": {"min": 443, "max": 443}}
  }]'
```

**Connectivity on-premises → OCI:**
- VPN: IPSec VPN via Customer Premises Equipment
- FastConnect: dedicated connection (1-10 Gbps)

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Network/Concepts/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como troubleshootar VM OCI que não boota após migração on-premises?

Troubleshootar VM que não boota pós-migração:

**Passo 1: Console history**
```
oci compute instance get-console-history \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID

oci compute instance get-console-history-content \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID \
  --console-history-id ocid1.consolehistory.oc1..exampleID
```

**Passo 2: Recovery mode**
```
# Detach boot volume
oci compute boot-volume-attachment detach \
  --boot-volume-attachment-id ocid1.bootvolumeattachment.oc1..exampleID

# Attach como data volume
oci compute volume-attachment attach \
  --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..recoveryID \
  --volume-id ocid1.bootvolume.oc1..exampleID \
  --type paravirtualized

# Investigar
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/recovery
cat /mnt/recovery/var/log/boot.log
cat /mnt/recovery/etc/fstab
cat /mnt/recovery/etc/cloud/cloud.cfg
```

**Problemas comuns:**

| Sintoma | Causa | Solução |
|---------|-------|--------|
| Kernel panic | Drivers VirtIO faltando | Instalar antes de exportar |
| No bootable device | Formato incompatível | Reconverter imagem |
| Hang no cloud-init | Configuração incorreta | Verificar cloud.cfg |
| Network não funciona | Network config | Verificar interfaces |
| fstab errors | Device names mudaram | Usar UUIDs |

**Corrigir fstab:**
```
sudo blkid
# Atualizar /etc/fstab com UUIDs
sudo sed -i 's|/dev/sda1|UUID=<uuid>|' /mnt/recovery/etc/fstab
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Tasks/accessingconsole.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como validar performance de VMs OCI após migração on-premises?

Validar performance pós-migração on-premises → OCI:

**Benchmark de CPU:**
```
sysbench cpu --cpu-max-prime=20000 --threads=4 run
```

**Benchmark de memória:**
```
sysbench memory --memory-block-size=1M --memory-total-size=10G run
```

**Benchmark de disco:**
```
# Read IOPS
fio --name=randread --ioengine=libaio --iodepth=32 --rw=randread \
  --bs=4k --direct=1 --size=1G --numjobs=4 --runtime=60

# Write IOPS
fio --name=randwrite --ioengine=libaio --iodepth=32 --rw=randwrite \
  --bs=4k --direct=1 --size=1G --numjobs=4 --runtime=60
```

**Benchmark de rede:**
```
iperf3 -c <oci-internal-ip> -t 30
ping -c 100 <oci-internal-ip>
```

**Checklist:**
- [ ] CPU performance dentro de 10% do baseline on-premises
- [ ] Disk IOPS atende requisitos
- [ ] Network latency dentro do esperado
- [ ] Application response times comparáveis

**Se performance inferior:**
- Verificar shape equivalente
- Testar com Block Volumes dedicados
- Verificar se attach é paravirtualized

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Concepts/computeoverview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como planejar cutover com mínimo downtime na migração on-premises para OCI?

Planejar cutover on-premises → OCI com mínimo downtime:

**Fase 1: Preparação**
1. Inventory completo
2. Mapear workloads para OCI
3. Provisionar ambiente OCI
4. Testar migração com VM non-prod

**Fase 2: Replicação de dados**
```
# Sync inicial
rsync -avz --progress /app/data/ user@oci-instance:/app/data/

# Para databases, usar replication nativa
# MySQL: master-slave
# PostgreSQL: streaming replication
```

**Fase 3: Cutover**
```
# 1. Maintenance mode
# 2. Parar writes on-premises
# 3. Sync final
rsync -avz --delete /app/data/ user@oci-instance:/app/data/

# 4. Iniciar OCI
oci compute instance action --instance-id ocid1.instance.oc1..exampleID --action START

# 5. Atualizar DNS
# 6. Validar aplicação
```

**Fase 4: Rollback plan**
- Manter on-premises por 48h
- DNS com TTL baixo

**Estimativa de downtime:**
- Com sync prévio: 15-30 minutos
- Sem sync: horas

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Migration/overview.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Como migrar scripts de automação on-premises para OCI?

Migrar scripts de automação on-premises → OCI:

**Mapeamento de ferramentas:**

| On-premises | OCI Equivalente |
|-------------|----------------|
| Ansible | OCI Ansible Modules |
| Terraform | OCI Terraform Provider |
| Shell scripts | OCI CLI + cloud-init |
| Jenkins | OCI DevOps Build Pipelines |
| Cron jobs | OCI Functions + Events |

**Exemplo: Script de provisioning**

Antes (on-premises):
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Criar VM no VMware
vmware-cmd -s register /vmfs/volumes/datastore/vm/vm.vmx
vmware-cmd /vmfs/volumes/datastore/vm/vm.vmx start
```

Depois (OCI):
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Criar instância no OCI
oci compute instance launch \
  --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID \
  --shape VM.Standard.E4.Flex \
  --subnet-id ocid1.subnet.oc1..exampleID \
  --display-name "nova-vm" \
  --metadata '{"user_data": "'$(base64 -w0 cloud-init.yaml)'"}'
```

**OCI CLI para automação:**
```
# Instalar OCI CLI
bash -c "$(curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh)"

# Exemplos de automação
oci compute instance list --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..exampleID
oci os object list --bucket-name meu-bucket
```

**Cloud-init para bootstrap:**
```yaml
#cloud-config
package_update: true
packages:
  - nginx
  - monitoring-agent
runcmd:
  - systemctl enable nginx
  - systemctl start nginx
```

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/cliconcepts.htm
You are an OCI specialist with expertise in on-premises to OCI migration. Provide technical guidance on VM migration to OCI Compute.
Quais são as principais diferenças entre VMs on-premises e OCI Compute?

Principais diferenças entre VMs on-premises e OCI Compute:

**1. Provisioning:**
- On-premises: semanas para hardware, horas para VM
- OCI: minutos para instância

**2. Scaling:**
- On-premises: capacity planning antecipado
- OCI: shapes flexíveis, auto-scaling

**3. Storage:**
- On-premises: SAN/NAS local
- OCI: Block Volumes, Object Storage, File Storage

**4. Networking:**
- On-premises: switches, routers físicos
- OCI: VCN, subnets, gateways virtuais

**5. Availability:**
- On-premises: HA com hardware redundante
- OCI: ADs + Fault Domains

**6. Pricing:**
- On-premises: CapEx (hardware) + OpEx (manutenção)
- OCI: Pay-as-you-go [MUTABLE]

**7. Management:**
- On-premises: vSphere, Hyper-V
- OCI: Console, CLI, Terraform, API

**8. Security:**
- On-premises: firewalls físicos
- OCI: Security Lists, NSGs, WAF

Referência: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Compute/Concepts/computeoverview.htm
